label: Supervised learning

node.label: Supervised learning
node.definition: A learner maps inputs to labeled targets using an explicit error signal.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Supervised learning is to connect Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences to downstream capabilities including Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Preference learning, Active learning & curriculum learning.
examples: substrate for Active learning & curriculum learning
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Labels, targets, rewards & preferences.

dependencies.rationale: Supervised learning uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Supervised learning uses Labels, targets, rewards & preferences as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Supervised learning uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
